info.title: FieldView Platform API
info.description:
All endpoints are only accessible via https and are located at
`api.platform.com`.
## Limits
If the request threshold for a partner is exceeded, a `429` response code will be returned.  Additionally, the following header will be returned: `Retry-After` (https://www.w3.org/Protocols/rfc2616/rfc2616-sec14.html).
## Pagination
Pagination is performed via headers.  Any request which returns a list may be paginated.  If the results are paginated, a header will be returned: `x-next-token`
## Response Headers
The following response headers will be returned on every call, successful or not: `x-http-request-id`.  If you every experience an issue with our api, and need to contact technical support, please supply the value of the `x-http-request-id` header along with an approximate time of when the request was made.

get.description: Gets all `Layer` objects to which the client has access.

parameters.name: Authorization
parameters.description: OAuth2 bearer token which identifies both the user and the client.  The token must be obtained via the 'Login with Climate Field View' flow.

parameters.name: x-next-token
parameters.description: Opaque string which allows for fetching the next batch of results.

parameters.name: bbox
parameters.description: Optional bounding box by which to filter the results. Value should be in json notation conforming to WGS84 Bounds [-180.0000, -90.0000, 180.0000, 90.0000]

parameters.name: startTime
parameters.description: Optional start time by which to filter layer results. Time should be in ISO 8601 format.

parameters.name: endTime
parameters.description: Optional end time by which to filter layer results. Time should be in ISO 8601 format.

get.description: Retrieves results from the specified layer. Results may be filtered spatially by supplying a bounding box (bbox) or temporally by specifing a start time (startTime) and/or end time (endTime).  An array of results will be returned.  If no results match, either due to filtering, or because the layer is empty, an empty array will be returned.  If the specified layer does not exist, a 404 will be returned.  If more than 100 results exists, the results will be paginated, ordered by modification time.

parameters.name: Authorization
parameters.description: OAuth2 bearer token which identifies both the user and the client.  The token must be obtained via the 'Login with Climate Field View' flow.

parameters.name: x-next-token
parameters.description: Opaque string which allows for fetching the next batch of results.

parameters.name: provider
parameters.description: name of the layer provider, (eg. deere, veris, climate, ...)

parameters.name: type
parameters.description: the type of the layer, (eg. soil, as-planted, ...)

parameters.name: bbox
parameters.description: Optional bounding box by which to filter the results. Value should be in json notation conforming to WGS84 Bounds [-180.0000, -90.0000, 180.0000, 90.0000]

parameters.name: startTime
parameters.description: Optional start time by which to filter layer results. Time should be in ISO 8601 format.

parameters.name: endTime
parameters.description: Optional end time by which to filter layer results. Time should be in ISO 8601 format.

403.description: Forbidden, the user/partner tuple is not authorized to retrieve the given layer.

post.summary: Upload data to the specified layer
post.description: Step one in uploading a data product to a layer.  The method will return a signed s3 url to which the caller may upload the data.

parameters.name: Authorization
parameters.description: OAuth2 bearer token which identifies both the user and the client.  The token must be obtained via the 'Login with Climate Field View' flow.

parameters.name: provider
parameters.description: The provider which owns the layer

200.description: Returns a signed s3 url to which the caller can upload data.

403.description: Forbidden, the partner may not upload data to the given layer.

put.summary: Update or correct data for a specific result.

parameters.name: Authorization
parameters.description: OAuth2 bearer token which identifies both the user and the client.  The token must be obtained via the 'Login with Climate Field View' flow.

parameters.name: provider
parameters.description: name of the layer provider, (eg. deere, veris, climate, ...)

parameters.name: type
parameters.description: the type of the layer, (eg. soil, as-planted, ...)

403.description: Forbidden, the user/partner tuple is not authorized to retrieve the given layer.

LayerType.description: The type of layer, eg (soil, as-planted, as-harvested)

provider.description: The client id of the partner who created the layer, eg (climate, solum, veris)

format.description: The native format of the layer.  Raster or Vector

url.description: A signed s3 url where the data may be retrieved from
